subject: Checking in on your work at Visa Ventures and Sardine
body:
Hi David,

Great reconnecting earlier this year. With Sardine's momentum in real-time fraud detection and Visa's expanding venture bets in agentic AI and identity, you're sitting at a fascinating intersection.

Having scaled SoyYo's fraud prevention platform to 3M+ users before its acquisition, I'm curious — what trends in AI-driven risk infrastructure are you finding most compelling right now?

Would love your perspective on where the space is heading.
reasoning: Focuses on David's unique dual vantage point across Visa Ventures and Sardine, ties Santiago's fraud-prevention credentials naturally, and asks for insight rather than a favor.

subject: Following up — Chris Gardner's intro and Payana's momentum
body:
Hi Matias,

Hope you're well. Chris Gardner from Underscore connected us a few months back, and I've been following Payana's growth in LATAM payments with real interest.

How are you navigating the embedded finance and AI shifts shaping B2B payments in the region?

Given my work at SMCU building fintech partnerships and my LATAM payments background, I'd love your perspective on where Payana sees the biggest strategic challenges ahead.

Best,
Santiago
reasoning: References the Chris Gardner intro as the shared connection, centers the email on Payana's strategic landscape in LATAM B2B payments, and asks for Matias's perspective rather than making any explicit ask about roles or referrals.

subject: Checking in — how's the startup advisory going?
body:
Hi Kevin,

Great connecting through Jay DeVivo earlier this year. I've been thinking about our conversation on Agentic AI and telco crossover.

How's the startup you're advising progressing — any traction with the AI roadmap? I took your advice on exploring Okta and the gateway space, which has been valuable.

As you evaluate Agentic AI solutions for your clients, would it help to compare notes on what I'm seeing in payments and fraud prevention?
reasoning: References their shared connection (Jay DeVivo), centers Kevin's advisory work and prior recommendations, and offers a collaborative exchange on Agentic AI rather than asking for leads.

subject: Checking in on Eastern Bank's digital momentum
body:
Hi Ashley,

Great to reconnect — I've been following Eastern Bank's expansion and digital banking push since the Rockland Trust competition heated up. How is the team navigating embedded finance and AI integration across your product lines?

On my end, I've been building agentic AI and payments solutions at a credit union, which has sharpened my perspective on community-focused innovation.

Would love your insight — is Eastern exploring fintech partnerships where a payments/AI leader could add value?
reasoning: References Eastern Bank's competitive landscape and digital strategy to show genuine interest, keeps focus on Ashley's world, and asks for insight rather than explicitly requesting a referral or job lead.

subject: Following up — 3rd Thursday connections and Nexxus fintech trends
body:
Hi Mark,

Hope you've been well since we last connected through David Rubinstein. I've been curious how things are evolving at The Nexxus Group — are you seeing more fintech firms lean into agentic AI or embedded payments this year?

On my end, I'm building AI and payments solutions at a credit union while distributing agentic AI platforms across LATAM.

Would love your perspective on where you see the biggest gaps in fintech leadership right now.
reasoning: References the David Rubinstein intro and 3rd Thursday ties for warmth, centers the email on Mark's market perspective at Nexxus Group, and asks for insight on fintech leadership gaps — positioning Santiago as a peer seeking advice rather than explicitly requesting referrals.

subject: Checking in — post-Rapyd chapter
body:
Hi Eric,

Hope you're well — Walt Cox connected us earlier this year. I've been curious how your independent work is evolving since Rapyd. The cross-border payments space is shifting fast with agentic AI reshaping fraud and CX workflows.

Given your depth in payments infrastructure, what trends are you most excited about heading into 2026?

Would love to reconnect — always value your perspective.

Best,
Santiago
reasoning: References the mutual connection (Walt Cox) early, centers Eric's post-Rapyd journey and expertise, asks for his perspective on trends rather than requesting a referral, and ties in Santiago's relevant domain without making it about him.

subject: Checking in on Pygma's momentum
body:
Hi Andrés,

Hope you've been well since Fernando connected us. I've been following Pygma's work supporting founders across LATAM — curious how the platform has evolved and what trends you're seeing in AI-native startups entering fintech.

On my end, I've been building agentic AI and payments solutions at a credit union while distributing Maven AGI across LATAM.

Would love your perspective — any emerging areas where our worlds might intersect?
reasoning: Pygma accelerates LATAM startups, so centering the email on their ecosystem trends and asking for Andrés's perspective keeps over half the content about him while naturally surfacing Santiago's relevant AI and fintech work.

subject: Following up on Agentic AI & payments conversation
body:
Hi Barnard,

Great connecting at the MIT CIO Symposium around Agentic AI and payments — that conversation stuck with me.

With the 2026 Symposium approaching, I'm curious how you're seeing the Agentic AI agenda evolve this year. Are enterprise payment use cases getting more traction in the programming?

I'm deepening my work in agentic AI for financial services and would value your perspective on where the biggest gaps remain.

Best,
Santiago
reasoning: Reconnects through their shared MIT CIO Symposium context, centers Barnard's evolving work on the event's agenda, and asks for insight on Agentic AI trends rather than requesting any direct lead.

subject: Checking in — Nubank's AI momentum
body:
Hi Samuel,

Hope you've been well since Felipe Castellanos connected us. Nubank's expansion into agentic AI-driven customer experiences has been impressive to follow — are you seeing that reshape internal priorities on your end?

I'm now building AI and fintech solutions at a U.S. credit union while distributing an Agentic AI platform across LATAM. Given your vantage point at Nubank, I'd value your perspective — would you have 15 minutes to reconnect?
reasoning: References the mutual connection early, centers the email on Nubank's AI initiatives and Samuel's perspective, and asks for advice rather than explicitly requesting a referral or job lead.

subject: Checking in — how are things evolving at SMCU?
body:
Hi Grace,

Hope you've been well since Pedro Arce connected us. I'd love to hear how your initiatives at St. Mary's Credit Union have progressed over the past few months — especially anything around member experience or new partnerships.

As someone deeply invested in agentic AI and payments innovation within credit unions, I'm curious: are there areas where you'd welcome a fresh perspective or collaboration?

Would enjoy catching up if you have a few minutes.
reasoning: This harvest-cycle check-in references the Pedro Arce connection, centers Grace's work and progress at SMCU, and gently opens the door to collaboration without explicitly asking for a job or referral.

subject: Checking in — Alumni Ventures and the agentic AI wave
body:

reasoning: Leverages the Giovanni Donaldson introduction, centers the email on Alumni Ventures' AI/fintech portfolio activity, and asks for Ron's perspective rather than explicitly requesting a referral or job lead.

subject: Checking in — Russell Reynolds and the AI leadership wave
body:

Would value your perspective on how boards are framing these hybrid profiles — any insights you'd share?

subject: Checking in — Sardine's momentum in fraud prevention
body:
